meta.title: Rail Safety Management System — Midland Metro Light Rail (non-mainline)
meta.subtitle: ROGS Part 3 verification route · ROGS/ORR element set · for acceptance
meta.author: HSE Rail
meta.classification: Internal — for submission / competent-person review required
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label (Accountable Duty-Holder, safety-critical roles, COSS). Identifiers and any Sentinel numbers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document. This SMS is submitted FOR acceptance; ORR acceptance is the Safety Authority's act and is not asserted here.

sections.text: Safety Management System for Midland Metro, a light-rail (tram) passenger operation. The dutyholder is a NON-MAINLINE operation, so the ROGS route is a Part 3 safety verification (not a mainline safety certificate or authorisation). This SMS is built to the ROGS 2006 (SI 2006/599) element set and is submitted FOR acceptance — it does not assert acceptance by ORR.

metrics.label: Open [GAP]s
metrics.caption: each closed by a SMART action

sections.text: Route resolved: a non-mainline (tram/metro/heritage) operation takes a ROGS Part 3 safety verification — NOT a mainline safety certificate (transport operator) or safety authorisation (infrastructure manager).

The operation's safety policy commits to operating the light-rail system so far as is reasonably practicable, with measurable objectives: zero tram-stop overruns, a year-on-year reduction in pedestrian/road-interface conflicts, and a competence-currency rate of 100% for safety-critical roles.

rows: Overall accountability for the SMS and the Part 3 verification
sections.caption: Accountabilities — role / accountability / status (named individuals de-identified to role labels)

rows.title: Tram / road-pedestrian interface conflict at street-running sections
rows.description: Shared street-running corridors create a struck-pedestrian / road-vehicle collision risk at crossings and stops.

rows.title: Tram-stop overrun on the descending gradient approach
rows.description: A wet-rail braking margin on the gradient approach to two stops.

sections.text: Hierarchy of controls (every mitigation ranked, SFAIRP)

rows.control: Segregate the highest-conflict corridor from road traffic where reasonably practicable (dedicated alignment)
Sanding / enhanced rail-conditioning + automatic-braking enforcement on the gradient approach
Approach speed restriction + driver wet-rail procedure on the affected stops

sections.text: No street-running conflict is left to a 'drivers take care' administrative control alone where a segregation or braking-enforcement (higher-order) control is reasonably practicable (SFAIRP).

items: A proposed extension to a new street-running section is a significant change — the CSM-RA significance test applies (Reg (EU) 402/2013, KB-REG-CSM-RA).
Risk acceptance demonstrated via reference systems (the existing comparable street-running corridor) plus explicit risk estimation for the new pedestrian interface.
An independent AsBo (Assessment Body) will assess the correct application of CSM-RA for the extension.

sections.text: Tram drivers and safety-critical roles hold current competence; fitness-for-duty is managed via the confidential occupational-health pathway (individual medical detail held in a separate confidential record, not in this SMS). Sentinel does not apply to this non-mainline operation; an equivalent tram-driver competence register is maintained.

Vehicle and infrastructure condition are managed under a planned maintenance regime; the Entity in Charge of Maintenance arrangements for the tram fleet are documented and the maintenance records are auditable.
Emergency preparedness covers evacuation at street-running stops, overhead-line isolation, and coordination with the local emergency services; exercise records are retained.
The SMS is monitored via near-miss reporting, a rolling internal audit programme, and a management review. The stated review cadence is annual or on significant change, whichever is first.
Incident learning and audit findings feed a continuous-improvement log; corrective actions are tracked to closure with role owners and due dates.

rows.action: Complete the segregation feasibility study for the highest-conflict street-running corridor
Commission the wet-rail braking-enforcement upgrade on the two gradient-approach stops

sections.text: This SMS is submitted FOR acceptance. ORR is the Safety Authority; acceptance is its act and is not asserted in this document.

items: Railways and Other Guided Transport Systems (Safety) Regulations 2006 (SI 2006/599) — non-mainline Part 3 safety verification — KB-REG-ROGS
Common Safety Method for Risk Evaluation and Assessment, Reg (EU) 402/2013 (retained EU law in GB) — KB-REG-CSM-RA
Decision-support only — a competent person (rail-SMS / ORR-aware) must review before use.
